assertions.text: A car pulled out in front of the narrator on the fast road.
The narrator was traveling around 110 km/h before emergency braking.
The narrator's car was clipped once (a single impact) by the other car.
The other car clipped the narrator's car from the front (pulled out in front) implying a frontal or side-front impact.
The emergency braking occurred before the impact.
After the impact, the narrator slowed to walking pace.
The collision was limited to a single clip (implying a relatively minor impact).

verdicts.evidence: The claim that "A car pulled out in front" is about another vehicle's manoeuvre and road position, which cannot be determined from the provided telemetry alone; the sensors show vehicle motion but do not identify another vehicle or its path. No timestamped external-object or camera data is present in the window (see sensor overview) [0.0](T1).

The narrator claimed about "around 110 km/h before emergency braking"; the GPS speed first sample is [113.0](T2) km/h at [0.0](T2) s, which is within ~15% of stated [110](T2) km/h, so the claim is supported.

Emergency braking is supported by a sustained deceleration: detect_events found a sustained accel/decel on axis accel_x from [0.22](T3) s to [4.11](T3) s with mean magnitude [0.55](T3) g and peak moving avg [−0.7](T3) g, and gps_speed drops from [113.0](T3) to [78.0](T3) km/h between [0.0](T3)–[3.0](T3), indicating heavy braking.

The narrator says there was a single clip impact, but the IMU and detect_events show no high-magnitude impact spike: accel_x peak_abs_dev is only [0.777](T2) g at [1.12](T2) s and detect_events did not flag any impact-spike detection (no |a-baseline|>2.5 g). That sensor evidence contradicts a distinct crash impact event.

Direction of an external object clipping (frontal or side-front) cannot be determined because device-frame axes are undocumented and IMU axes were not mapped to vehicle longitudinal/lateral; accel peaks exist on axis x (peak at [1.12](T2) s) but that does not uniquely identify a frontal impact.

Telemetry shows the major deceleration begins before the IMU peak: sustained braking on accel_x starts at [0.22](T3) s and the largest accel_x peak occurs at [1.12](T2) s; GPS speed drops from [113.0](T2) to [78.0](T3) by [3.0](T3) s, consistent with braking preceding any pulse in the window.

The narrator says they slowed to walking pace after impact; gps_speed last recorded value at [15.0](T2) s is [42.0](T2) km/h — this is faster than typical walking pace but GPS shows later minima as low as [8.0](T2) km/h during the window; the claim "walking pace" is therefore partially supported by the presence of low speeds down to [8.0](T2) km/h but the final value at [15.0](T2) s is [42.0](T2) km/h, making the exact phrasing ambiguous.

The claim that the collision was limited to a single minor clip is contradicted by the absence of a distinct impact pulse: no crash-pulse exceedance was detected and accel peaks are modest (peak_abs_dev [0.777](T2) g, integrated device-axis delta-V on accel_x [−51.8](T2) km/h is large but represents braking), indicating there was not a clear collision impulse matching a clip.

result.rationale: GPS and IMU clearly show heavy braking from [113.0](T2) km/h and sustained deceleration ([0.55](T3) g mean, peak moving avg [−0.7](T3) g) with speed drops, but no high-g impact spike was detected to corroborate a single clip; direction and external vehicle maneuvers are unverifiable from available channels.

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
